name: Beer-Batter Onion Rings
recipeIngredient: 2 large White onions, sliced into 1/4-inch thick rings
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the flour, salt, and pepper.
2. Add the egg yolks and oil to the dry ingredients. Mix until well combined.
3. Gradually whisk in the beer until the batter is smooth and pourable.
4. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 3-1/2 hours or overnight.
5. Preheat the oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to 375°F (190°C).
6. Slice the onions into rings, about 1/4-inch thick.
7. Dip each onion ring into the batter, ensuring it is fully coated.
8. Carefully place the battered onion rings into the hot oil and fry until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
9. Remove the onion rings from the oil using a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
10. The batter can also be used for other vegetables or fish fillets by dipping and frying as described above.
